work_order.objective:
Project: aetherion-maze (maze-groundup HTML5 canvas). Input: A single composite reference-board image showing terrain, walls, ground, decorations, hero (12 frames), enemies (skeleton/goblin idle+walk), items (chest, key, potion, shield, crystal), traps (spikes), portal, effects, and UI icons. This is NOT a machine-usable sprite sheet.

Your task (deliver concise, production-ready artifacts):
1) Asset packaging plan (ASSET_PACKAGING.md):
   - State clearly that the current image is a reference board, not runtime-ready.
   - Define the final delivery options and pick a preferred default:
     Option A (preferred): Loose transparent PNGs organized in folders.
     Option B: Uniform 64x64 grid atlas (power-of-two canvas) + JSON with rects.
     Option C: Texture atlas PNG + JSON (arbitrary rects).
   - Specify naming conventions, folder layout, tile size=64, and required role coverage: walls (h,v,tl,tr,bl,br,endcap,door), ground (dirt, grass), decorations (tree_cluster, rock_cluster, bush, flowers), hero (idle/walk x4 dirs), enemies (skeleton idle/walk, goblin idle/walk), items (chest, key, potion, shield, crystal), traps (spikes), effects (portal, speed_bolt, magic_orb), UI (heart, coin, key_icon, shield_icon).
   - Include exact file paths we will expect under assets/painted/… and explicit atlas JSON schema if B/C is chosen.
2) Missing asset list (FILELIST template):
   - Provide a line-by-line checklist of required PNGs (64x64) with target paths and notes (e.g., collision, pivot if needed).
3) Atlas JSON template (atlas_spec_template.json):
   - If B or C: provide a complete JSON template with keys, including sample rects (0,0,64,64), tile_size=64, and properties such as layer and type.

Constraints & guidance:
- Only floor and walls belong to TileMaps. All other objects are entities/layers.
- We must not rely on a click-mapper. Catalog/manifest-first.
- The end product must allow immediate drop-in for our HTML5 canvas loader.

Output format:
- Begin with a one-paragraph asset-readiness verdict.
- Then the ASSET_PACKAGING.md content.
- Then a FILELIST (plain text) block.
- Then atlas_spec_template.json content.
- Keep concise and unambiguous.

delivery_model.strategy_label: Lead -> Creative -> Game Dev -> App Dev

stage_guidance.operator: Own scope, freeze the delivery target, and keep the handoffs honest.
stage_guidance.creative-dev: Define the creative target, vision guardrails, and artifact acceptance language before implementation drifts.
stage_guidance.game-dev: Own the playable build path and converge the actual game implementation on the chosen target artifact.
stage_guidance.app-dev: Own the app, tooling, portal, and review-loop support needed to make the chosen artifact reviewable and repeatable.

handoff_contract: freeze one primary delivery target before downstream implementation expands scope
treat creative-dev as the vision and acceptance guardrail for downstream lanes
treat game-dev as the owner of the playable implementation path once the vision is frozen
treat app-dev as the owner of portal, tooling, packaging, and review-loop support after the build path is clear
require each lane to leave a concrete handoff for the next lane instead of sending the operator back into open-ended review

verification_required: classify each referenced art input as reference board, loose sprites, uniform grid sheet, atlas plus manifest, or unknown before integrating it
if an image includes labels, section dividers, notes, or a presentation background, state that it is a reference board and not a build-ready runtime sheet
do not claim sprite integration is complete until a machine-usable artifact exists such as loose transparent sprites, a uniform grid sheet with proven cell geometry, or an atlas with manifest
work_order.project_now: Lead orchestration complete for: Converge Aetherion Maze under one lead-owned path that runs creative-dev first, then game-dev, then app-dev, with one primary delivery target and no parallel rebuild drift.
work_order.project_next: Operator selects the next execution slice from the orchestration artifact and keeps one primary delivery target.

structured_result.summary: Asset-readiness verdict: The supplied image is a labeled reference board (a presentation sheet), not a build-ready runtime spritesheet. It mixes categories, likely has baked labels/backgrounds, and lacks consistent transparent bounds and per-sprite metadata. We will not integrate it directly. The packaging below converts it into a manifest-first, loader-ready set with 64x64 RGBA sprites and clear roles/layers.
implemented: Wrote packaging plan, file checklist, and atlas template into the creative-dev workspace:
Adopted kebab-case naming, 64×64 tiles, 2 px safe borders, center anchors per loaded workflow skill.
verified: Files created at the above paths.
assumed: The board includes the listed subjects; enemies do not require 4-direction variants (hero does).
Loader accepts the proposed atlas JSON keys (layer, type, frames, aliases). If not, we will adapt the schema.
risks: If the loader expects underscores or different keys, we’ll need a quick mapping pass.
If art comes without 2 px padding, atlas bleed/mip issues can occur.
If enemies require directional facings, the current FILELIST will need expansion.
next_actions: Implement the HTML5 canvas loader bindings for Option A paths using FILELIST as the manifest.
If delivering an atlas (B/C), wire atlas_spec_template.json fields to the loader.
Convert the reference board into the listed PNGs, preserving 2 px safe borders and center anchors.
Add a quick validator to ensure each delivered PNG is 64×64 and file names match kebab-case.
artifacts: ASSET_PACKAGING.md, FILELIST, atlas_spec_template.json in the creative-dev workspace (paths above).
Yes — to game-dev to integrate the manifest-first loading and verify layer roles (TileMap only for ground/walls).

"summary": "Packaged Aetherion Maze art into a manifest-first plan: Option A loose 64x64 PNGs (preferred), with explicit folder layout, roles, and naming; plus FILELIST checklist and an atlas JSON template for B/C. The reference board is flagged as non-runtime and will be converted per this plan.",
"Artifacts written to the creative-dev workspace paths listed above"
"Enemies do not require 4-direction variants by default",
"Loader will accept the provided atlas JSON schema with minimal or no changes"
"Sprites delivered without 2 px padding may cause bleeding",
"Directional facings for enemies may be required later"
"Game-dev: Wire the loader to Option A folder structure using FILELIST as the catalog (no click-mapper).",
"If using B/C: Bind atlas_spec_template.json fields (layer, type, frames) and test hero-walk-n animation.",
"Art: Extract sprites from the board to 64x64 PNGs with 2 px safe borders and center anchors.",
"QA: Add a validator to assert 64x64 dims, kebab-case names, and presence of required files."
